entries.source: Microsoft Security Blog, Snyk, Socket.dev, Unit 42, Google Cloud Threat Intel
entries.description: Compromised npm releases of axios 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 pulled in malicious dependency plain-crypto-js that dropped a cross-platform RAT via postinstall. Malicious versions were live for ~3 hours on 2026-03-31 before being yanked.
entries.attribution: North Korea-nexus (Mandiant / Google Cloud Threat Intel)

entries.description: Malicious package injected as a fake dependency by compromised axios versions. Its postinstall script (setup.js) deploys a cross-platform RAT with reversed-base64 + XOR obfuscation (key: OrDeR_7077, constant: 333).

Account takeover of ua-parser-js maintainer led to publication of versions containing a cryptominer (jsextension) and credential stealer targeting Linux and Windows. Affected versions installed preinstall.js that downloaded OS-specific binaries.

Maintainer-initiated sabotage. colors 1.4.1+ added an infinite loop printing 'LIBERTY LIBERTY LIBERTY' (zalgo text), breaking thousands of downstream projects. While not malware per se, it demonstrates supply chain risk from maintainer compromise and the versions should be avoided.
Same maintainer sabotage as colors. faker 6.6.6 replaced all module exports with a zalgo text infinite loop.

Social engineering attack: new maintainer gained publish rights, added flatmap-stream as a dependency. The payload targeted Copay Bitcoin wallet users, stealing wallet credentials via an AES-encrypted payload that only activated when imported by Copay.
Purpose-built malicious package injected into event-stream@3.3.6. Contained AES-encrypted payload targeting the Copay Bitcoin wallet, activated only when the specific dependency tree was detected.

Typosquat of chalk. Part of a September 2024 wave of ~40 malicious packages impersonating chalk, debug, and related popular packages. Contained postinstall scripts that exfiltrated environment variables and downloaded second-stage payloads.
Typosquat of chalk from the same September 2024 wave.
Typosquat of debug from the September 2024 wave. Same exfil + second-stage payload pattern.
Another typosquat from the September 2024 chalk+debug wave.
Malicious fork of the legitimate noblox.js Roblox API wrapper. Contained credential-stealing code targeting Roblox session tokens and Discord tokens via webhook exfiltration.
Another malicious noblox.js fork variant targeting Roblox and Discord credentials.
Yet another malicious noblox.js fork variant.

notes: This file ships with a starter set and is expanded as new campaigns are documented.

Priority additions for future updates: coa (2021), rc (2021), eslint-scope (2018), bootstrap-sass (2019), rest-client (Ruby, but pattern reference), PhantomRaven cluster packages from Phylum research, Shai-Hulud worm packages from Aikido.dev/Checkmarx writeups.
For wildcard versions ('*'), flag the package name regardless of installed version — the package itself is entirely malicious with no legitimate versions.
